description: Sector overlay for U.S. telecommunications carriers, broadband, wireless, VoIP, network operations, CPNI, licensing, outage, and lawful-intercept documentation.

metadata.scope: United States sector overlay controls for Telecommunications. Intended to be used with us/core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from U.S. federal statutes, regulations, agency guidance, regulator materials, and sector frameworks available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance. State, territorial, tribal, and local obligations may impose additional requirements.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Communications Assistance for Law Enforcement Act materials
FCC outage reporting and network reliability materials

rules.title: FCC authorization, carrier, license, or registration evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for FCC authorization, license, registration, carrier status, spectrum authorization, or broadband/telecom service evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: CPNI privacy, authentication, and safeguard evidence is documented
rules.summary: Checks for CPNI, customer proprietary network information, authentication, customer approval, disclosure safeguards, or annual certification evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: CALEA lawful-intercept capability evidence is present where carrier obligations apply
rules.summary: Checks for CALEA, lawful intercept, law-enforcement assistance capability, intercept compliance, or network capability evidence.

rules.title: Network outage reporting and reliability controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for NORS, outage reporting, network reliability, incident notification, redundancy, or emergency communications evidence.

rules.title: 911/E911 service obligations are documented where voice service is described
rules.summary: Checks for 911, E911, emergency calling, location information, VoIP 911, or public safety answering point evidence.
